rulings.Case Title: THENG TIENG BOO VS. STEVEN OU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 27, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT STEVEN OU, XIAN LIU Motion To End Case is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient Notice per CCP 1005 and CCP 1013. Motion filed on March 20, 2023 and served by mail on March 17, 2023 for a hearing on March 28, 2023. There are only 7 court days between March 28, 2023 and March 17, 2023; and only 6 court days between March 20, 2023 and March 28, 2023. Required notice is 16 court days, plus 5 calendar day for mail. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ANGELO D''LIMA ET AL VS. VERTEX PROPERTY GROUP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Under Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16 (Anti-Slapp) To Strike The Complaint Of Plaintiffs Angelo D Lima And Juliane Smith
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 28, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT VERTEX PROPERTY GROUP Notice Of Motion And Motion Under Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16 (Anti-Slapp) To Strike The Complaint Of Plaintiffs Angelo D Lima And Juliane Smith is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. "At the first step, the moving defendant bears the burden of identifying all allegations of protected activity, and the claims for relief supported by them. If the court determines that relief is sought based on allegations arising from activity protected by the statute, the second step is reached." Baral v. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal.5th 376. Defendant's Notice of Motion while claiming that the motion seeks to strike "specified claims" from the complaint, does not actually specify any claims (See Notice of Motion). The Points and Authorities appear to specify the claims as "breach of contract" and "nuisance" (Points and Authorities 8:10-11) and identify the allegations (Points and Authorities 2:14-4:1). Based on the language chosen to identify these allegations as "claims," the moving party appears to conflate the two. Reading the Notice and Points and Authorities as a whole, Defendants have met their burden of identifying the allegations and the claims for relief arising therefrom only as to the claim for breach of contract which in its entirety arises out of protected activity. (Complaint, paragraph 51, "posting the 10-day notice on Plaintiff's door."). Plaintiffs failed to carry their burden to show of prevailing on the merits. Therefore, the claim for breach of contract arising out of the service of the Notice is stricken. Defendants failed to meet their burden to identify the claims for nuisance arising from protected activity. ANTI-SLAPP is framed by the pleadings, claims of nuisance are not based on any protected activity identified by the Defendants. (Complaint, paragraph 82 and 88). It is Defendants burden under the first step of the ANTI-SLAPP to tie the allegations to the claims, for nuisance claims Defendants failed to do so. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 200 PAUL, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. UNITEDLAYER, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO FILE UNDER SEAL PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT AND EXHIBITS THERETO
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 27, 2023 line 4. PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO FILE UNDER SEAL PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT AND EXHIBITS THERETO is DENIED without prejudice. Declaration in support of the motion is conclusory and does not support the request as to all portions of the complaint sought to be sealed.
